short: Drafting a full plan up front, then carrying out each step in a separate execution pass.
definition: Plan-and-Execute separates high-level planning from step-by-step action. A planner model first decomposes a complex goal into an ordered list of steps; an executor then works through each one, often calling tools, while a controller can revise the plan if problems arise. Splitting the two phases reduces myopic decisions and improves reliability on long-horizon tasks compared with purely reactive loops.
